near4.decision: Which CI provider should the org standardize on?
near4.context: Code is on GitHub; builds are Rust and Node; some jobs need beefier runners; one platform engineer.

near4.decision: Which infrastructure-as-code tool should the new platform team adopt?
near4.context: AWS-only today, multi-cloud unlikely but possible; team prefers real programming languages but has Terraform experience.

near4.decision: Where should application secrets live?
near4.context: Twelve services on AWS, three environments, no dedicated security engineer.

near4.decision: How should feature flags be managed?
near4.context: Two product teams, ~20 flags today, growth expected, budget-conscious.

near4.decision: Which search backend should power the product's full-text search?
near4.context: Two million documents, moderate query volume, relevance tuning matters, small ops team.

near4.decision: What should front the public APIs?
near4.context: Five APIs, need rate limiting and auth termination, one infra engineer.

near4.decision: How should mobile beta builds reach testers?
near4.context: React Native app, 40 external beta testers, weekly builds.

near4.decision: What should the public documentation site run on?
near4.context: Markdown source in the repo, versioned docs needed, contributors are engineers.

near4.decision: Where should private packages be published?
near4.context: Eight internal JS libraries, GitHub-hosted code, cost-sensitive.

near4.decision: How should on-call alerts be routed?
options: A rotation spreadsheet plus Slack webhooks
near4.context: Six engineers sharing on-call, Grafana already deployed, alert fatigue is a current complaint.

near4.decision: Which orchestrator should the data pipelines move to?
near4.context: Fourteen nightly jobs with some dependencies, one data engineer, failures currently detected manually.

near4.decision: How should user sessions be stored?
options: Hybrid: short JWT plus refresh in Redis
near4.context: Need instant revocation on logout, ~50k daily actives, Redis already in the stack.

near4.decision: Which state management should the growing React app adopt?
near4.context: App has outgrown prop drilling; four frontend developers with mixed preferences; heavy async data.

near4.decision: Which payments provider should the SaaS integrate?
near4.context: B2B SaaS selling globally, subscriptions plus usage overage, wants merchant-of-record option considered.

near4.decision: Which transactional email provider should the product use?
near4.context: ~200k emails/month, deliverability matters, developer experience valued, cost-sensitive.

near4.decision: Where should the team's internal knowledge base live?
options: A wiki directory in the monorepo
Google Docs with a shared drive
near4.context: Twenty-five people, engineering-heavy, docs currently scattered across all four.

near4.decision: What should the technical interview's main evaluation be?
options: A live pairing session on real code
near4.context: Hiring two seniors; candidate pool includes people who refuse take-homes and people who freeze live; false hires are very costly.

near4.decision: Which localization approach should the app adopt before its EU launch?
near4.context: React app, three target languages, one release engineer, launch in eight weeks.

near4.decision: How should the fine-tuned model be served for inference?
near4.context: 7B model, spiky traffic, p95 latency target 800ms, one ML engineer.

near4.decision: Which vector store should the retrieval feature use?
near4.context: Three million embeddings, moderate QPS, Postgres already operated, wants filtering by metadata.

near4.decision: Where should encrypted database backups be stored?
options: An on-prem NAS in the office
near4.context: Nightly 40GB dumps, 90-day retention, restore speed matters occasionally, cost matters monthly.

near4.decision: What should the company's work-location policy become?
options: Each team chooses its own policy
near4.context: Sixty people across two cities plus eight remote hires; leases up for renewal; attrition risk cited in surveys.

near4.decision: How should the new pricing page be validated?
options: A/B test with a experimentation tool
Ship it and watch the funnel
near4.context: ~3k weekly signups, effect sizes expected small, engineering time for testing infra is limited.

near4.decision: What cadence should incident post-mortems follow?
options: One review per incident within 48 hours
A weekly rollup of the week's incidents
near4.context: Roughly six incidents monthly, mostly minor; review fatigue is real; two repeat sev-2s last quarter.

near4.decision: How should code ownership be declared?
near4.context: Twenty-two engineers, review latency complaints, two knowledge-silo incidents last quarter.

near4.decision: Which database migration tool should the Rust service standardize on?
near4.context: Postgres, sqlx already the query layer, CI-driven deploys, two backend engineers.

near4.decision: What shell should the desktop companion app use?
options: A PWA instead of a desktop app
near4.context: Web team knows TypeScript; app is a lightweight tray utility; binary size and memory matter to users.

near4.decision: What should run the scheduled background jobs?
options: System cron on the app host
near4.context: Thirty scheduled jobs, a few need retries and visibility, currently failing silently on cron.

near4.decision: How should the team allocate its conference budget for the year?
options: Send six engineers as attendees with talks submitted
Skip conferences; spend on content marketing
near4.context: First developer-tools product year; no conversion data from any channel yet; budget fixed.

near4.decision: How should flaky tests be handled in CI?
options: Quarantine tag with a weekly fix rotation
Auto-retry failures up to three times
Block merges until the flake is fixed
Delete flaky tests and rely on the rest
near4.context: Suite of 4k tests, ~1% flake rate, merge queue backing up, two recent real bugs initially dismissed as flakes.
